_note: Generation taxonomy for the offline bank (T035 / FR-205, research.md R2). This is the INPUT to bank/generate.py — it describes WHICH archetypes to bulk-generate, not the questions themselves (those are written by Bedrock at generation time). Two-axis taxonomy: 1 General competency family + 6 Domain/Industry families (>= 5 required), each spanning competency x question_type x difficulty. generate.py expands every family into (family x competency-entry x difficulty) generation slots, so re-running with a higher --limit widens coverage deterministically. Competency/question_type/category/difficulty values are the closed enums from data-model.md / research.md R5 and MUST satisfy the question_archetype CHECK constraints.

families.label: General competencies (cross-role behavioral / situational)
families.domain_context: competencies every role shares regardless of industry; ground in the candidate's general experience and the target role's expectations, never in domain-specific technical knowledge
themes: a tight or shifting deadline
a disagreement or conflict with a colleague
ambiguous or incomplete direction
a setback, mistake, or failure you owned
influencing someone without formal authority
juggling competing priorities

families.label: Software Engineering
families.domain_context: debugging production issues, system and API design, build-vs-buy and speed-vs-maintainability trade-offs, testing and code review, incident response, reasoning about failure modes
themes: debugging a hard production issue
a design or architecture trade-off
testing, code review, or code quality
a build-vs-buy or speed-vs-maintainability decision
an incident or outage you helped resolve
working with legacy code or technical debt

families.label: Data & Analytics
families.domain_context: data quality and validation, turning analysis into decisions, communicating uncertainty to non-analysts, handling ambiguous or contradictory data, basic statistics and reporting
themes: catching or handling a data-quality problem
turning an analysis into a business decision
communicating uncertainty to a non-analyst
ambiguous or contradictory data
building a metric, dashboard, or report someone relied on
a result that surprised stakeholders

families.label: Product Management
families.domain_context: prioritisation under constraints, defining success metrics, balancing user needs against engineering cost, saying no to stakeholders, discovery and validation of a feature
themes: prioritising under hard constraints
defining or defending a success metric
saying no to a stakeholder
discovery or validation of a feature
balancing user needs against engineering cost
a launch or bet that did not go as planned

families.label: Sales & Business Development
families.domain_context: qualifying a prospect, handling objections, recovering a stalled deal, balancing short-term targets against long-term client trust, communicating value rather than features
themes: qualifying or disqualifying a prospect
handling a tough objection
recovering a stalled or lost deal
short-term target versus long-term client trust
communicating value rather than features
missing or fighting to hit a number

families.label: Nursing & Healthcare
families.domain_context: patient prioritisation and triage, communicating with distressed patients and families, escalating a safety concern, working under protocol while exercising judgement, handling a deteriorating patient
themes: prioritising or triaging competing patients
communicating with a distressed patient or family
escalating a safety concern
exercising judgement within or against protocol
responding to a deteriorating patient
a mistake or near-miss you helped catch

families.label: Finance & Accounting
families.domain_context: explaining a variance, ensuring accuracy under deadline pressure, flagging a control or compliance concern, translating numbers into a business recommendation, handling incomplete financial data
themes: explaining or investigating a variance
ensuring accuracy under deadline pressure
flagging a control or compliance concern
translating numbers into a business recommendation
working with incomplete or messy financial data
pushing back on a number or assumption
